outlook: Gold closed the week at $4,430 after tagging $4,475 intraday — the highest print since the March 10 sell-off. Safe-haven flows are the dominant driver right now, with the Middle East conflict keeping a bid under gold even as the dollar firms. The setup favours bulls above $4,380, and a weekly close above $4,500 would open the door to retest $4,630.
watching: I'm watching whether gold can gap above $4,475 on the Sunday reopen. If we get a gap-up open with follow-through into the $4,500 handle during the Asia session, that tells me the weekend risk premium is expanding — and I'll be looking at how the London session responds to that level. If $4,475 gets rejected again, I want to see $4,380 hold as the floor.
trade_context: The macro setup is a tug of war: geopolitical risk is pushing gold higher, but a firming dollar at 100.21 and 10-year yields near 4.43% are capping the upside. The COT report shows managed money still net long 168K contracts — positioning is stretched but not extreme. The play is to respect $4,380 support and trade the range until $4,500 breaks decisively. Invalidation below $4,316 (200-day zone).
platform_signal: Daily ATR regime flagged elevated volatility — 1.3x the 20-day average. The signal engine is scanning for a momentum breakout above $4,500 on the 4H timeframe. Subscribers can see the live regime status on the dashboard.
